name: Toffee Fudge Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F. Grease and flour a 9-inch square baking pan.
2. Combine the Heath bars and walnuts in a work bowl. Chop coarsely using 6 to 8 on/off turns of an offset spatula. Set aside.
3. In another work bowl, combine sugar and chocolate. Mix for 6 on/off turns, then process until the chocolate is as fine as the sugar, about 1 minute.
4. Add butter and blend for 1 minute. Stir in eggs, vanilla, and salt, blending until fluffy, about 40 seconds, stopping to scrape down sides if necessary.
5. Add flour and the Heath bar mixture, blending with 4 to 5 on/off turns until the flour is incorporated but not overprocessed. If needed, gently mix any remaining lumps with a spatula.
6. Pour batter into the prepared pan and spread evenly. Bake until a tester inserted in the center comes out almost clean, about 50 minutes (for firmer, cakelike brownies, bake an additional 5 minutes).
7. Allow to cool in the pan on a rack. Cut into 1 3/4-inch squares.
8. Store in an airtight container. These are dark, moist, and crunchy with bits of toffee candy.
